items.title: Copper: Pullback draws Chinese buying interest – ING
items.summary: ING strategists Ewa Manthey and Warren Patterson say Copper prices on the LME are retreating after Monday’s rally that followed a temporary pause in US strike plans on Iran.

items.title: NZD: RBNZ cautious on conflict inflation – BNY
items.summary: BNY's Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage relays comments from RBNZ Governor Anna Breman, who expects the Middle East conflict to lift near‑term headline inflation and weaken New Zealand’s growth momentum.

items.title: ECB's Sleijpen: Energy prices likely to become entrenched more quickly than in 2022
items.summary: European Central Bank policymaker Olaf Sleijpen said on Tuesday that the rising energy prices are likely to become entrenched in wider economy more quickly than they did during the 2022 energy crisis, as reported by Reuters.

items.title: Euro area: War impact weighs on outlook – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Senior Economist Dr. Vincent Stamer notes that the Euro area composite PMI fell from 51.9 to 50.5 in March, with services weakening and manufacturing flattered by longer delivery times. He links this to the war in Iran, which is hurting expectations and lifting input prices.

items.title: GBP: Inflation stalling complicates BoE path – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities’ Global Strategy Team expects UK inflation to hold at 3.0% year-on-year in February, in line with the Bank of England (BoE) and market consensus. Core CPI is seen steady at 3.1%, with services easing but core goods ticking higher.

items.title: WTI Price Forecast: Holds key 20-day EMA amid Middle East conflicts
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), futures on NYMEX, is up 2% to near $90.00 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The oil price holds early gains, driven by Iran’s refusal to be involved in any direct talks with the United States (US) regarding the end of conflicts in the Middle East.

items.title: USD: Weaker as conflict risk eases – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lee Hardman notes the US Dollar remains under pressure after a sharp sell-off linked to de-escalation in the Middle East. The Dollar index again failed to clear 100.00 and retreated toward 98.88.

items.title: HUF: War risk delays rate cuts – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank analyst Michael Pfister notes that despite low Hungarian inflation and February’s initial 25 bp cut, the Iran war has shifted priorities, with markets and the bank expecting the central bank of Hungary, Magyar Nemzeti Bank (MNB) to hold rates today.

items.title: Oil: Geopolitics keeps pressure on supply chains – Rabobank
items.summary: Rabobank’s Senior Macro Strategist Bas van Geffen notes that Iran’s control over the Strait of Hormuz and ongoing regional tensions are keeping risks elevated for Oil and broader energy markets.

items.title: Dow Jones futures gain on reports of Iran’s approval for negotiations
items.summary: Dow Jones futures inch higher 0.9% to above 46,550 during European hours on Tuesday, ahead of the US cash market open. Meanwhile, S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures gains 0.14% and 0.23% to near 6,650 and 24,460, respectively, at the time of writing.

items.title: BoJ: April hike expected after soft data – Danske Bank
items.summary: Danske Research Team notes softer Japanese data, with composite PMI slipping and core CPI falling below target for the first time in four years, largely due to fuel subsidies. Despite this, PMIs show rising input prices and a weak Japanese Yen.

items.title: DXY: Range top holds as headlines steer trade – ING
items.summary: ING’s Chris Turner notes the Dollar has softened after comments on possible negotiations with Iran, but sees limited downside while the Strait of Hormuz remains shut and energy prices are elevated.
